message:
fix(core): add find command safety analysis to symbolic executor

The symbolic executor previously treated find as an ordinary command,
only checking path arguments for traversal. However find has dangerous
action flags (-delete, -exec, -execdir, -ok, -okdir) that can execute
arbitrary commands or perform unbounded deletions — even when the
search path itself is within the workspace boundary.

Changes:
- Add FIND_EXEC_FLAGS constant listing find's command-execution actions
- Implement check_find_safety() that flags:
  - -delete as ViolationCategory::UnboundedDeletion (destructive)
  - -exec/-execdir/-ok/-okdir as ViolationCategory::BannedBinary
    (opaque command execution)
- Hook find into the main analyze loop with dedicated handling:
  check_find_safety + check_path_args + check_redirects, bypassing
  the generic safe_set check since find needs both path and action
  validation

Tests:
- safe_find_within_cwd: find src -name '*.rs' is permitted
- unsafe_find_parent_traversal: find ../shared catches PathTraversal
- unsafe_find_delete: find -delete catches UnboundedDeletion
- unsafe_find_exec_rm: find -exec rm catches BannedBinary

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
